paper_title: Technology from hunting blinds: A study of lithic assemblages in Late Holocene stone structures from Patagonia (Argentina)

methods.description: Analysis of lithic assemblages from stone structures to understand the frequency and types of artefacts, including tools, debitage, and cores, based on Aschero's guidelines.

steps.description: Collect lithic assemblages including tools, debitage, and cores from inner and outer surfaces of structures, as well as from excavations.
steps.searchable_summary: collect lithic assemblages from archaeological sites

steps.description: Exclude undifferentiated tools and distal flakes missing the platform to avoid biases.
steps.searchable_summary: filter out undifferentiated tools and incomplete flakes

steps.description: Measure variables such as the frequency of raw materials in debitage, cores, and tools. For debitage, also measure length as a size indicator.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze frequency and size of lithic materials

steps.description: For Cerro Pampa, analyze a sample of 30% of the obsidian debitage due to the large number recovered.
Calculate the proportion of tools and cores per structure by dividing the total of tools and cores by the number of analyzed structures with lithic materials in each area.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate proportion of tools and cores per structure
